title: 10 best herbal creams for bloating

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with bloating because of its natural digestive properties. The menthol in peppermint has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to bloating. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help to break down food more efficiently, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced gas buildup. By soothing the digestive tract and promoting healthy digestion, herbal peppermint creams provide relief from uncomfortable bloating symptoms, leaving you feeling lighter and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Irritant properties: The menthol content in peppermint cream stimulates digestion and relieves bloating by irritating the digestive tract, which helps to stimulate the movement of food through the system.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to bloating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in peppermint cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other essential oils, peppermint roots help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with bloating.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort caused by bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita, carrier oil, and beeswax for the recipe.
Combine one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves with two tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the essential oils are released from the peppermint.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix one teaspoon of beeswax with two tablespoons of the infused oil and heat in a double boiler until melted and combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with bloating because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the abdomen, while also helping to stimulate digestion and promote the elimination of toxins. As a result, herbal ginger creams can provide fast and effective relief from bloating, leaving you feeling lighter, more comfortable, and with a renewed sense of digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulation: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which stimulate digestive enzymes to break down proteins and carbohydrates more efficiently, reducing bloating and discomfort.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Gastric Motility Regulation: The bioactive compounds in herbal ginger creams can regulate gastric motility, promoting regular bowel movements and preventing stagnation or backup of food and gas in the intestines, which can contribute to bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help alleviate symptoms of bloating.
Roots: Contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for soothing bloated bellies.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 10 grams of dried ginger, 20ml of sweet almond oil, and 10ml of beeswax.
Infuse the dried ginger in 100ml of hot water for 10 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the infused water and mix it with the sweet almond oil and melted beeswax.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 20 minutes until it reaches a smooth and consistent texture.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool to room temperature before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with bloating because of its unique combination of properties that work together to alleviate digestive discomfort. The herbal extract in fennel cream soothes and relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to bloating. Additionally, fennel's natural carminative agents help to break down and eliminate gas in the intestines, further reducing bloating and discomfort. By promoting healthy digestion and eliminating toxins, fennel creams provide long-lasting relief from bloating and other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel cream helps to reduce bloating by releasing trapped gas in the digestive system, relieving pressure and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing cramping, spasms, and inflammation that can contribute to bloating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate bloating and discomfort caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and mucilage, which helps to soothe digestive issues and relieve bloating symptoms.
Seeds: Contains essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 millilitres of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours, then strain and discard solids before whipping with a hand blender until creamy consistency is achieved.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with bloating because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal issues. By providing a protective barrier for the stomach lining and reducing spasms in the intestines, herbal licorice creams can help alleviate bloating and discomfort, promoting a more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of bloating such as abdominal discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice creams have been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can help alleviate bloating and other digestive issues.

Isomaltose: This natural sweetener has prebiotic properties, meaning it helps feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms of bloating such as gas and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues related to bloating.
Barks: Licorice barks have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by bloating.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids that help to reduce gas and alleviate symptoms associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground root powder with 4 tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 15-20 minutes or until the wax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f to 180°f on a thermometer.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and mixing well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks or at room temperature for up to 3 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with bloating because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which ease digestive discomfort and reduce swelling in the abdominal area. The curcumin present in turmeric has natural digestive enzymes that stimulate digestion, relieve constipation, and soothe irritated stomach lining, reducing water retention and gas buildup. This results in a significant reduction in bloating, leaving you feeling lighter, more comfortable, and confident in your skin.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: The primary active compound in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
Gingerol: A bioactive compound found in ginger, gingerol has natural carminative properties that can help ease digestion, reduce flatulence, and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
Fennel seed oil: Fennel seed oil contains estragole, a compound that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that may contribute to bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in antioxidants and have natural antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing digestive discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Grind one teaspoon of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the turmeric powder with three tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small bowl.
Add five drops of peppermint essential oil and two drops of ginger essential oil to the mixture for their digestive benefits.
Stir the mixture well and let it steep at room temperature for 24 hours to allow for infusion and blending of ingredients.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, squeezing gently to extract as much cream as possible before discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with bloating because its soothing properties can calm inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with bloating. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to relax the muscles in the intestines, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced gas and bloating. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can also alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of bloating. By applying topically, chamomile creams provide targeted relief for bloated skin and digestive discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles of the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can cause cramping, bloating, and discomfort.
Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin and flavonoids in chamomile have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a popular choice for making medicinal creams as they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm digestive issues.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are renowned for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground chamomile flowers with 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f to 170°f.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer for 10 minutes or until it forms a light and fluffy texture.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and seal them tightly, allowing the mixture to solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with bloating because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause gas and discomfort. The creams also help to stimulate digestion and improve absorption of nutrients, which can alleviate constipation and bloating. remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion cream helps reduce bloating by increasing urine production, which can alleviate water retention and ease discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream may help soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), that can contribute to bloating.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties may help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a natural prebiotic that helps regulate digestion and reduce symptoms of bloating.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 20ml of jojoba oil and 5ml of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 2% of tea tree essential oil by volume while stirring gently for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and store them at room temperature allowing it to cool and thicken completely for 24 hours.
Once cooled and thickened, use the taraxacum officinale cream as needed, applying a thin layer to affected areas 2-3 times daily or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with bloating because they contain compounds that stimulate digestion and improve gut motility. remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried citrus x aurantium peel, calendula flowers, and sweet almond oil.
Combine 20g of dried citrus x aurantium peel with 10g of calendula flowers in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in 100ml of hot water for 5 minutes to create a tea-like infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and setting liquid aside.
Mix 20ml of the liquid with 10ml of sweet almond oil, adding 2 drops of lemon essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out one hundred grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and place them in a heat-resistant glass container.
Combine the dried flowers with one hundred milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and stir well to ensure even distribution.
Heat the mixture using a low-temperature double boiler method, maintaining a temperature between 60 and 70 degrees celsius for four hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another heat-resistant container, discarding the solids after pressing firmly on the solids to extract all liquid.
Pour the infused oil into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for at least six weeks before use.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Mix 20% ginkgo biloba extract powder with 80% coconut oil in a double boiler until smooth.
Add 5% beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even distribution of ingredients.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely before storing in a dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves measure out 100 grams for processing.
Dry the harvested salvia officinalis leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for 3 days.
Combine dried salvia officinalis leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Infuse the mixture for 2 weeks shaking the container daily and then strain the liquid.
Mix the infused liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of coconut oil then heat.

Barks: Contains natural astringent properties that can help soothe and calm irritated intestines, reducing bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the eucalyptus powder with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a small bowl, stirring well for 3 minutes to ensure even distribution.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping with an electric whisk for 2-3 minutes until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with bloating because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and relax the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and rinse them with cool water.
Combine the leaves with 1/4 cup of organic coconut oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling them to the brim and allowing it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with bloating because of its unique blend of natural ingredients, particularly cinnamaldehyde, a potent compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive system. remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground cinnamomum verum with 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a fragrant infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature for 1 hour.
Whip the cooled oil with 10g of beeswax and 2ml of vitamin e oil in a mixing bowl until smooth and creamy, then fill sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers of borago officinalis and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers with enough vodka to create a 1:4 ratio and steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the extracted liquid with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1 part extract to 2 parts wax and oil.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with bloating because of its ability to relax the smooth muscle lining in the digestive tract. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers.
Grind the herbal material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 6 tablespoons of coconut oil with 4 tablespoons of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of ground gaultheria procumbens to the melted oil and wax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh juniperus communis leaves and 1 cup of dried leaves in a clean environment.
Combine the fresh and dried leaves in a large pot filled with 4 liters of distilled water, boil for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids after 60 minutes.
Add 1 cup of sweet almond oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax to the liquid, heating the mixture in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping into a smooth cream consistency using an electric mixer for 10 minutes.

The herb's active compounds, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, work to soothe irritated tissues and relax muscle contractions that can contribute to bloating. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestive regularity, feverfew creams can help alleviate symptoms of bloating, leaving you feeling more comfortable and confident in your own skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like parthenolide, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Antispasmodic effects: The herbs in feverfew creams have antispasmodic properties, which help relax muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can contribute to bloating and discomfort.

Digestive soothing agents: Feverfew creams contain soothing agents like flavonoids, which help calm irritations and reduce inflammation in the digestive system, promoting a healthy gut environment and alleviating symptoms of bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate bloating symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the feverfew plant are rich in parthenolide, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried and before noon sun exposure.
Dry the plant material at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent spoilage and preserve flavonoids and terpenes.
Crush 50g of dried tanacetum parthenium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix the powdered tanacetum parthenium with 200ml of olive oil and 10% glycerin at room temperature for 2 hours for optimal infusion.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to sterilized containers, allowing it to mature for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with bloating because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties of the yarrow plant. The cream's active ingredients help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating. Additionally, yarrow's carminative properties help to expel gas and ease pressure in the abdominal area, providing rapid relief from bloating and discomfort. By promoting healthy digestion and relieving discomfort, herbal yarrow creams can offer natural and effective relief from bloating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow creams help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms associated with bloating and discomfort. This reduces the pressure and tension that can exacerbate bloating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort. By decreasing inflammation, yarrow creams can help alleviate symptoms.

Digestive-soothing properties: Yarrow has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate bowel movements and reduce symptoms of bloating such as gas and cramping. This soothes the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and promoting regularity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with bloating.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce bloating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for infusion in 250ml of boiling water.
Steep the infused mixture for 15-20 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 30g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil to create a cream base.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bloating are natural topical treatments infused with plant-based ingredients that help to soothe and calm digestive discomfort. These creams work by applying a gentle, non-invasive pressure to the abdominal area, stimulating the body's natural elimination processes and reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating. Examples of herbal creams that help with bloating include peppermint and ginger-based products, which can be applied directly to the stomach to alleviate symptoms like cramping and gas.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bloating with herbal creams is by using natural ingredients that soothe digestive issues. Look for creams containing marshmallow root, which calms inflammation and reduces bloating. Ginger and peppermint are also effective in reducing nausea and discomfort. Apply the cream topically to the abdomen area, massaging gently to help stimulate digestion. Regular use can provide long-term relief from bloating symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bloating that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These creams can also help to reduce redness and itching associated with these conditions, promoting healthy and clear skin.
